Attorney General Jeff Jackson Takes Office

On Jan. 1, 2025, Attorney General Jeff Jackson became the 51st Attorney General of North Carolina.

 About Attorney General Jackson

Protect Yourself After Hurricane Helene

Our hearts are with the people of western North Carolina. Unfortunately, scammers will stop at nothing to take advantage of people, even those who want to help.

 Learn more here

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Restores $165 Million for North Carolina Public Schools

After Attorney General Jackson sued, millions in public education funding that had been frozen by the federal government were restored for North Carolina classrooms. 

 Learn more

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Takes Action to Protect Children from Harmful Deepfake and AI Content

Attorney General Jeff Jackson is leading bipartisan efforts with attorneys general across the country to protect children from predatory AI and block profits from deepfake imagery. 

 Learn more

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Marks Hurricane Helene Anniversary

NCDOJ colleagues volunteered to build homes during the one-year anniversary of Hurricane Helene. The work is supported by AmeriCorps funding that Attorney General Jackson won back to aid western North Carolina recovery. 

 Learn more

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Shut Down Fraudulent Cancer Charity Operator

The scam charity misled donors and raised millions that never went to charity. 

 Learn more

Featured News

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Leads Phase 2 of Operation Robocall Roundup, Announces Investigation of Major Service Providers Inteliquent, Bandwidth, Lumen, and Peerless

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Commemorates 24th Anniversary of September 11 Attacks

Attorney General Jeff Jackson Opposes HPE/Juniper Merger Over Corruption Concerns

Consumer Alert: Don’t Fall for Jury Duty Scams
